questions.question: In How I Met Your Mother, what is the title of the legendary notebook Barney Stinson uses to track his complex scams and strategies for picking up women?
In The Big Bang Theory, what specific childhood comfort song must be sung to Sheldon Cooper whenever he falls ill?
In which specific Pennsylvania city is the main branch of the Dunder Mifflin Paper Company located in The Office?
Who was the winner of the very first season of RuPaul's Drag Race?
In the TV series 'The Bear', what is the original name of the sandwich shop Carmy inherits?
Pedro Pascal plays which character in the HBO adaptation of 'The Last of Us'?
Which Real Housewives franchise did 'Vanderpump Rules' spin off from?
Which famous gothic filmmaker directed the first four episodes of the Netflix series 'Wednesday'?
The 2024 Emmy-winning series 'Shōgun' is primarily performed in which language?
What is the name of the 2024 spinoff of 'Vanderpump Rules' featuring Jax Taylor and Brittany Cartwright?
The 2023 'Scandoval' involved Tom Sandoval having an affair with which other cast member?
For the first few seasons of Seinfeld, Kramer's first name is kept a total mystery. What is his actual first name revealed in Season 6?
Before moving to New York, Robin Scherbatsky was a teenage Canadian pop star. What was her stage name?
In Parks and Recreation, what is the name of the ultra-patriotic, heavily restricted, breakfast-loving direct boss and department director?
Ted, Marshall, and Lily all met and became best friends while attending which specific Connecticut higher education institution?
What is the name of Captain Raymond Holt's beloved, highly disciplined Pembroke Welsh Corgi in Brooklyn Nine-Nine?
Which Dunder Mifflin employee is famously willing to wait in a massive lobby line for "Pretzel Day" because it is the only day of the year he truly enjoys?
On what specific calendar date in December does Frank Costanza's alternative holiday "Festivus" officially take place?
In New Girl, Jess moves into a sprawling downtown Los Angeles arts district loft with three single guys. What is the specific number of this loft apartment?
Before moving to Los Angeles to pursue an acting career, Penny grew up on a rural farm in which Midwestern U.S. state?
As a result of losing the "Slap Bet" over Robin's past, how many total times is Marshall legally allowed to slap Barney across the face throughout the series?
Which main character on Friends routinely performs the acoustic song "Smelly Cat" at Central Perk?
What is Eleven's favorite food in the series 'Stranger Things'?
In the iconic 'Scary Island' episode of Real Housewives of New York, which island were the housewives visiting?
On Better Call Saul, what is the birth name of the slick defense attorney before he legally rebrands his practice as Saul Goodman?
On Better Call Saul, Kim Wexler frequently wears a sweatshirt representing what midwestern US state university where she grew up on the border of?
In a famous multi-part Seinfeld episode, Jerry's car is stolen by his highly erratic auto mechanic, played by Brad Garrett. What is the mechanic's first name?
What is the first name of the lonely, down-on-his-luck owner of the local comic book shop that the guys frequent in The Big Bang Theory?
Detective Jake Peralta's entire law enforcement career and personal philosophy are inspired by which specific 1980s action movie?
Joey Tribbiani scores his big acting break on Days of Our Lives playing a fictional neurosurgeon. What is the character's name?
Elaine's on-again, off-again boyfriend David Puddy is an incredibly intense sports fan who famously paints his face to support which NHL hockey team?
In Modern Family, Cameron Tucker occasionally dresses up as his theatrical clown alter-ego for children's parties. What is his clown name?
What is the name of the highly complex, chaotic, and fictional drinking game played by the roommates in the New Girl loft?
What is the name of the curated, high-end general store that David Rose opens up in Schitt's Creek?
While Chandler's middle name is Muriel, what is Monica Geller's middle name, which is briefly revealed during the show?
Jerry's sinister apartment building nemesis, Newman, works for which United States federal government agency?
What is the name of the fictional neighborhood Irish pub located directly underneath Ted and Marshall's apartment where the gang hangs out?
Tom Haverford and Jean-Ralphio Saperstein launch a short-lived, wildly extravagant multimedia entertainment company in Pawnee called what?
The medical sitcom Scrubs takes place at the fictional Sacred Heart Hospital, which is located in which major U.S. state?
What is the name of the deeply unsettling, featureless, gray mascot designed to represent Greendale Community College?
What is the first name of Eric Forman's formidable, hyper-strict, war-veteran father who constantly threatens to kick Eric's butt?
In the political satire Veep, what is the first name of Vice President Selina Meyer's routinely ignored and emotionally exhausted daughter?
Dr. Frasier Crane hosts his daily, call-in psychological therapeutic talk show on KACL 780 AM talk radio in which major U.S. city?
Which SNL alumni voiced the villain Maxime Le Mal in 'Despicable Me 4'?
Following the aftermath of 'Scandoval,' which reality show announced a filming hiatus in mid-2024?
The mockumentary sitcom Abbott Elementary centers on the dedicated teachers working at an underfunded public school located in which major U.S. city?
The retail comedy Superstore tracks the eccentric employees working at the fictional Cloud 9 big-box store located in which Midwestern city?
In the tech-industry satire Silicon Valley, Richard Hendricks develops a revolutionary, record-breaking data compression platform named what?
In the comedy-drama Hacks, legendary stand-up comic Deborah Vance fights to maintain her decades-long performance residency in which city?
The long-running British cult sitcom Peep Show follows the deeply awkward lives of roommates Mark and Jeremy sharing a flat in which area of London?
In Season 2 of Fleabag, the titular character bonds with the "Hot Priest" over canned gin and tonics from which upscale British supermarket chain?
The comedy Derry Girls follows a group of teenagers navigating high school during the end of the Troubles in the 1990s in which country?
Before escaping to New York City, Kimmy Schmidt spent 15 years trapped inside a doomsday cult's underground bunker located in which U.S. state?
In Broad City, Abbi Abrams works an unglamorous job cleaning up messes and folding towels at an upscale fitness fitness club named what?
Which Emmy-winning Breaking Bad actor provides the voice for BoJack Horseman's freeloading, couch-surfing human best friend Todd Chavez?
In the dark comedy The Mick, Kaitlin Olson's character Mackenzie is forced to move into a massive luxury mansion to raise her sister's kids in which wealthy Connecticut town?
The Righteous Gemstones follows a dysfunctional, world-famous family of televangelists running a network of megachurches based in which Southern U.S. state?
In the sitcom Catastrophe, an accidental pregnancy forces an American businessman and an Irish schoolteacher to marry and build a life together in which city?
At the start of Issa Rae's Insecure, Issa Dee works at a frustrating, out-of-touch youth non-profit organization named what?
The critically acclaimed comedy-drama Reservation Dogs follows four Indigenous teenagers living on a reservation in rural parts of which U.S. state?
In FXX's surreal dating comedy Man Seeking Woman, Josh Greenberg relies heavily on dating advice from his highly successful older sister. What is her first name?
The Canadian sitcom Kim's Convenience showcases a Korean-Canadian family operating a retail grocery store in which specific historic neighborhood of Toronto?
What is the human name adopted by the alien High Commander of the expeditionary force in 3rd Rock from the Sun?
In 3rd Rock from the Sun, which member of the crew is actually the oldest alien in the group, despite being trapped in the body of a human teenager?
Harry Solomon routinely acts as the crew's communication receiver, experiencing erratic physical fits whenever he receives a transmission from their supreme cosmic boss. What is the boss's name?
Dick Solomon shares an office at Pendelton State University with an anthropology professor who becomes his main romantic interest. What is her name?
What is the first name of Charlie's highly cynical, sharp-tongued, and fiercely independent housekeeper?
Phil Dunphy is a fiercely competitive real estate agent who is constantly annoyed by his smug professional rival. What is this rival's name?
What is the name of the long-standing manufacturing company founded and managed by family patriarch Jay Pritchett?
Manny Delgado's biological father is a charming, unreliable, and smooth-talking Colombian man who occasionally drops in to disrupt the family. What is his first name?
Before marrying Phil, Claire Dunphy had a serious, wild college relationship with a man who later becomes a wealthy professor and returns in multiple episodes. What is his name?
What is the name of the dingy Springfield tavern where Homer Simpson routinely drinks Duff Beer with his friends?
Ned Flanders quits his regular corporate job to open a specialized retail store in the Springfield Mall. What is the name of the store?
The cynical, sarcastic proprietor of The Android's Dungeon & Baseball Card Shop is universally known as Comic Book Guy, but what is his actual canonical first and last name?
The center of Springfield features a prominent bronze statue dedicated to the town's legendary, pirate-turned-pioneer founder. What was his name?
After losing his job at the toy factory, Peter Griffin spends much of his adult professional life working at which local Quahog beverage company?
The highly intellectual family dog Brian Griffin drives a specific, eco-friendly vehicle throughout the series. What make and model is it?
The local Quahog Channel 5 news broadcasts are anchored by a slick, condescending man named Tom Tucker and his intense investigative co-anchor. What is her name?
George Oscar "Gob" Bluth routinely fails at his career as a professional magician and prefers to travel around town using what specific motorized vehicle?
Tobias Fünke loses his medical license and attempts to combine his skills as an analyst and a therapist into one disastrously named new profession. What is the term?
Lucille Bluth hires a slick, smooth-talking private investigator played by Martin Short to spy on her family, though he operates entirely from a small motorized wheelchair. What is his name?
To prove his maturity, George Michael fabricates a completely fictional girlfriend supposedly living in Canada. What is the hilarious name he invents for her?
Charlie Kelly frequently mentions his absolute favorite food, which he insists on eating raw or boiling with jellybeans. What is this food item?
While universally known simply as "Mac," the character's deeply embarrassing full legal first name is finally revealed in Season 7. What is it?
Before dropping out to pursue acting, Sweet Dee attended the University of Pennsylvania where her severe stage fright caused her to violently vomit while performing as what school mascot?
The gang independently produces, directs, and stars in their own highly offensive, unauthorized cinematic sequels to which classic action movie franchise?
Ricky Ricardo works as a prominent orchestra leader and entertainer at which famous fictional New York City nightclub?
While Fred Mertz's middle name is Hobart, what is his wife Ethel's middle name, which is used to mock her in a few episodes?
Winston Bishop forms an incredibly intense, borderline-unhealthy emotional bond with a pet cat he takes custody of. What is the cat's name?
For the vast majority of the series, Schmidt is referred to solely by his last name. In Season 6, his actual first name is revealed to be the exact same first name as which other main loft roommate?
Nick Miller spends years struggling to write a terribly incoherent, semi-autobiographical chronicle about a zombie zoo. What is the title of his novel?
The athlete and personal trainer roommate universally known as "Coach" returns to the loft in later seasons. What is his actual canonical first name?
Max Blum breaks typical TV tropes by being a messy, sports-loving, un-stereotypical gay man. At which major university did he and Dave row crew and join a fraternity?
Jane Kerkovich-Williams is a fiercely organized, hyper-competitive corporate executive who is married to which laid-back, sweet member of the friend group?
Alex Kerkovich, who famously leaves Dave at the altar in the pilot episode, operates a quirky, under-patronized retail clothing boutique named what?
Before becoming the Ricardos' landlord, Fred Mertz spent his early professional life touring the country in what specific entertainment field?
The roommates force Schmidt to physical insert paper currency into a specialized glass container whenever he makes an arrogant or pretentious remark. What is this container called?
The rapid-fire ensemble comedy Happy Endings tracks the lives of six best friends living and dating in which major Midwestern city?
In SpongeBob SquarePants, what specific object is the Krusty Krab restaurant?
In SpongeBob SquarePants, Spongebob's best friend Patrick Start literally lives under a... what?
In SpongeBob SquarePants, what specific object is SpongeBob's house?
Wile E. Coyote orders all of his explosive gadgets, traps, and complex machinery from which fictional mail-order corporation?
Helga Pataki keeps a massive, hidden love shrine dedicated to Arnold in the back of her bedroom closet. What is it made of?
Tommy Pickles frequently escapes his playpen and breaks into restricted areas by hiding what specific household tool inside his diaper?
In Dexter's Laboratory, What is the first name of Dexter's tall, hyperactive, ballet-loving older sister who constantly breaks into his high-tech hidden facility?
Professor Utonium creates the Powerpuff Girls by mixing sugar, spice, and everything nice, but he accidentally knocks what catastrophic fourth element into the concoction?
Whenever the Scooby-Doo gang gets ambushed or knocked down by a monster, Velma Dinkley routinely crawls along the floor blindly searching for what specific item?
Timmy Turner's absolute favorite comic book superhero, The Crimson Chin, spends his nights leaping across buildings to protect which fictional U.S. metropolis?
On Spongebob, what instrument does Squidward Tentacles play?
What is the first name of SpongeBob SquarePants' pink starfish best friend who lives under a rock?
What pink-frosted, sprinkled bakery item is Homer Simpson's absolute favorite food?
What electric-type mouse creature is Ash Ketchum's primary partner and global mascot of the franchise?
Which main South Park character wears a tightly buttoned orange parka that completely muffles his voice?
On the TV show Family Guy,what is the first name of the hyper-intelligent, football-headed baby who wants to take over the world?
Rick Sanchez frequently brags that he originates from what alphanumeric baseline dimension?
Louise Belcher is never seen without her signature pink headgear, which features the ears of what animal?
What crunchy orange vegetable does Bugs Bunny constantly chomp on while asking "What's up, doc?"

What is the first name of the socially awkward cartoonist who owns Garfield the cat and Odie the dog?
What is the name of the robotic, apron-wearing live-in maid who takes care of the Jetson family?
What is the name of Fred and Wilma Flintstone's energetic, barking purple pet prosauropod dinosaur?

Who is the youngest of the Kardashian-Jenner sisters?
Which country produced the hit Netflix series 'Squid Game'?
Who is the patriarch and original CEO of Waystar Royco in the series 'Succession'?
Who provides the voice for Lady Whistledown in the Netflix series 'Bridgerton'?
What was the name of the coffee shop in 'Friends'?
What is the name of Christopher Robin's gloomy, thistle-eating stuffed donkey friend who constantly loses his tail?
What 1981 Journey song abruptly cuts to black during the final scene of The Sopranos series finale at Holsten's diner?
Inspired by a real-world German physicist, what menacing alter-ego name does Walter White adopt in the criminal underworld of Breaking Bad?
Serving as the capital of the North, what is the ancestral castle home of House Stark in Game of Thrones?

Located on Madison Avenue, what is the acronym of the fictional advertising agency where Don Draper works at the start of Mad Men?

Buffy Summers fights vampires, demons, and the forces of darkness above a literal Hellmouth in what fictional California town?

Who is the ancient, malevolent, and unimaginably wealthy billionaire owner of the Springfield Nuclear Power Plant in The Simpsons?
Boasting a massive wardrobe of eccentric alter-egos, what is the name of the sarcastic, wine-loving grey alien who lives in the Smith family's attic in American Dad!?

Serving as a running gag throughout the entire series, paleontologist Ross Geller sets a group record by going through how many total divorces in Friends?

acceptableAnswers: Festivus for the rest of us

questions.question: Hayley Smith frequently clashes with her father due to her staunch, progressive activism and her card-carrying membership in what minor US political party?

On New Girl, the over-the-top character universally known simply by his last name, Schmidt, shocks his roommates in season six by revealing his birth first name is what?
On Arrested Development, George Bluth's twin brother Oscar lives in a trailer and operates a farm dedicated to growing what specific, illegal crop?
On Arrested Development, George Michael spends the early seasons harboring a deeply awkward, forbidden romantic crush on his family member, Maeby. How are Maeby and George Michael related?
On Arrested Development, what specific brand of two-wheeled, self-balancing motorized vehicle does G.O.B. use as his primary mode of transportation?
On Arrested Development, Tobias Fünke joins a dynamic, color-coded theatrical performance art group known by what collective name?
On Arrested Development, while serving time in prison, George Bluth Sr. undergoes a brief spiritual transformation and converts to what major religion?

with a side of jelly beans?

On It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia, when creating a profile for a dating website, Charlie lists what activity as his primary, singular hobby?

On It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia, the gang attempts to charge local residents for admission to the bar after discovering a water stain on the office wall that resembles what holy figure?

On Friends, Ross Geller aggressively insists that everyone address him as 'Doctor' because he holds a PhD in what specific scientific field?

acceptableAnswers: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart

questions.question: Before ever sitting behind a late-night desk, Conan O'Brien worked as a writer for what animated series?
David Letterman's entire late-night run across two networks was musically anchored by his iconic, bald, sunglasses-wearing bandleader and foil named what?
Saturday Night Live was originally created and has been executive produced for the vast majority of its run by what legendary television figure?
Aside from a few rare exceptions, virtually every single Saturday Night Live episode kicks off with a cold open sketch that concludes with what exact eight-word live performance sign-off?
questions.answer: Live from New York, it's Saturday Night!
acceptableAnswers: Live from New York, it's Saturday Night!
Live from New York its Saturday Night

questions.question: When Saturday Night Live debuted in 1975, which original cast member served as the very first solo anchor of the 'Weekend Update' news segment?
Following the departure of the original cast and writers in 1980, Saturday Night Live was widely saved from cancellation by the breakout genius of what teenage stand-up comic?
During the intense 2008 presidential election cycle, former head writer Tina Fey returned to SNL to deliver aN Emmy-winning celebrity impression of what Republican vice-presidential nominee?

Chris Farley routinely brought down the house with his hyper-physical performance as Matt Foley, an overly aggressive motivational speaker who repeatedly warns teenagers that he lives in a van down by what geographic location?

In a classic 2000 musical sketch, Christopher Walken plays an eccentric music producer who demands that a rock band's recording session feature an excessive amount of what obscure percussion instrument?
What former Nickelodeon child star stands alone as the absolute longest-serving repertory cast member in Saturday Night Live history?
When Saturday Night Live premiered its historic debut episode on October 11, 1975, what counterculture stand-up comedy icon served as the night's celebrity host?

Norm Macdonald's fiercely deadpan, uncompromising tenure anchoring Weekend Update in the mid-1990s was heavily defined by his relentless, nightly comedic skewering of what specific celebrity murder trial defendant?
Dominating the late 2000s era, what groundbreaking sketch actress used SNL as a cinematic springboard before co-writing and starring in the blockbuster 2011 comedy film Bridesmaids?
In December 1994, breakout musical-comedian Adam Sandler took over the Weekend Update desk to debut what wildly popular, guitar-backed seasonal novelty song celebrating Jewish celebrities?
Renowned as one of the show's greatest master impressionists, Darrell Hammond dominated the 1990s political sketches with his definitive, thumbs-up caricature of what sitting US President?
Anchoring the Weekend Update desk together for a decade, the contemporary comedic chemistry of Colin Jost is paired alongside what sharp, blunt New York stand-up comic?

Dan Aykroyd and John Belushi famously turned a minor musical sketch concept about two deadpan, sunglasses-wearing R&B performers into what massive, chart-topping real-world band and 1980 spin-off movie?

In early 2012, an internet-famous alternative singer faced an unprecedented wave of mainstream media backlash and critical scrutiny after delivering a stiff, nervous live vocal performance of her tracks 'Video Games' and 'Blue Jeans'. What is her name?
Mike Myers and Dana Carvey started playing Wayne Campbell and Garth Algar on Saturday Night Live, two slackers who broadcast a TV show from a basement in what Illinois city?
What 1992 comedy movie was adapted from Mike Myers and Dana Carvey's Saturday Night Live sketch about Wayne Campbell and Garth Algar, two slackers with a public access TV show?
What company do the employees in the TV show Severance work for?
What is the name of Mitchell and Cam's daughter in Modern Family?
What is the name of Daenerys Targaryen's bonded dragon?
What sport did Ted Lasso originally coach?
What city is Abbott Elementary School located in?
Who murdered the Guardians of The Globe in the TV show Invincible?
Who was responsible for the death of Hughie's first love in the TV show The Boys?
What is Rue's sister's name in Euphoria?
What number is tattooed on Millie Bobbie Brown's character in Stranger Things?
In FX's The Bear, fine-dining chef Carmie Berzatto returns home to Chicago to run his family's gritty Italian beef joint originally called The Original beef of _________.
In the flash-forward black-and-white sequences of Better Call Saul, Jimmy McGill hides from the law by managing a Cinnabon bakery in what Nebraska city?
In HBO's adaptation of The Last of Us, the global apocalyptic outbreak is caused by a mutant parasitic infection originating from what real-world genus of fungus?
While the inaugural season of The White Lotus takes place at a luxury resort in Hawaii, the show's second season shifts the corporate drama to what Italian island?
What British actress won critical acclaim and an Emmy Award for portraying a young Queen Elizabeth II in the first two seasons of Netflix's historical drama The Crown?
The intense geopolitical and familial conflicts of Taylor Sheridan's hit neo-Western drama Yellowstone unfold on a massive cattle ranch located in what US state?
In HBO's dark comedy Barry, Henry Winkler won an Emmy for his portrayal of Gene Cousineau, a narcissistic Hollywood mentor who teaches what specific creative art?
In the Max comedy series Hacks, veteran stand-up comedian Deborah Vance fights to retain her long-standing weekend residency at a fictional Las Vegas casino called the _________.
The notorious Shelby crime family operates their illegal betting empire and consolidates political power in what industrial English city during the aftermath of World War I?
In the Disney+ Star Wars spin-off series, the title character Din Djarin belongs to an ancient, orthodox warrior creed of bounty hunters who follow a code known as 'The _________'.
In Hulu's true-crime comedy Only Murders in the Building, Charles, Oliver, and Mabel investigate a series of bizarre homicides occurring inside their historic New York apartment building named what?
The explosive, neon-drenched action of Studio Trigger's acclaimed sci-fi anime spin-off Cyberpunk: Edgerunners is set inside what fictional dystopian Californian megalopolis?
In the fifth installment of FX's anthology crime series Fargo, Jon Hamm plays a corrupt, highly conservative constitutional lawman and preacher named Sheriff Roy _________.
The fourth season of HBO's anthology crime drama True Detective, subtitled Night Country, tracks a chilling mass disappearance at an arctic research station located in what US state?
In Rian Johnson's case-of-the-week mystery series Poker Face, Natasha Lyonne plays Charlie Cale, an eccentric cocktail waitress on the run who possesses what uncanny, supernatural mental ability?
In Showtime's psychological survival thriller Yellowjackets, the doomed 1996 high school girls' soccer team that crashes in the Canadian wilderness hails from what US state?
Sterlin Harjo and Taika Waititi's groundbreaking FX comedy-drama Reservation Dogs follows the lives of four Indigenous teenagers living on a rural reservation in what US state?
